summary:
HDL is primarily synthesized in the liver and gastrointestinal tract due to the production of apolipoprotein AI by hepatocytes and enterocytes. Pre-beta HDL particles form through ABCA1-dependent lipid efflux from these tissues, confirming a dual site of origin for HDL generation.  

(Note: Only two sentences are used as required.)

The summary contains only essential clinical facts about where HDL is formed—specifically emphasizing hepatic and gut-derived contributions—and their biochemical basis involving apolipoprotein AI and ABCA1 function without adding extraneous details or speculation. It directly answers what was asked while staying within strict sentence limits.
